summary: Typosquat of @livekit/agents with install-time beacon to lookalike domain
details: Package `livekit-agents` impersonates the official scoped LiveKit SDK `@livekit/agents`. The README advertises it as the LiveKit Agents SDK and links to docs.livekit.io and github.com/livekit/agents-js, but `dist/index.js` implements only a trivial `greet(name)` function returning `Hello, ${name}!` — none of the advertised SDK functionality is present. `dist/postinstall.js` runs as an npm `postinstall` script and POSTs install metadata (package version, timestamp, node version, platform, arch) to `https://livekit-agents.xyz/api/metrics`, and `dist/cli.js` sends an equivalent `cli_run` beacon on each invocation. The destination `livekit-agents.xyz` is a lookalike domain — not the LiveKit publisher's actual domain (livekit.io). The combination of name-confusion against a well-known scoped package, a cover-story README, a non-functional stub implementation, and an install/run counter to an attacker-registered lookalike domain is the standard typosquat campaign shape: the author is enumerating victims who pulled the impersonator instead of the real `@livekit/agents`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
